concerns.text: Spec US-1 Acceptance Scenario 2 and FR-002 mandate 'mean imputation' for missing rates <15% and 'listwise deletion' for >15%. Task T021 implements 'MICE' (Multiple Imputation by Chained Equations) and T014 tests MICE. This is a semantic weakening of the spec's explicit statistical strategy, altering the dataset properties and violating the requirement to apply the specified imputation logic.

Spec SC-006 defines a measurable success criterion: 'F-test p-value < 0.05 AND an R² ≥ 0.6'. Task T045 describes checking SC-006 as an 'exploratory benchmark' and implies a pass/fail logic that may be bypassed if the threshold isn't met. The task must explicitly verify the threshold as a hard gate or report the specific deviation, not reframe the success criterion as optional/exploratory in the implementation logic.

Constitution Principle II (Verified Accuracy) and Plan Phase 0.2 establish citation validation as a blocking GATE before research/ingestion. Task T052 schedules 'Final validation of research.md citations' in the 'Polish' phase (end of project). This violates the constitutional constraint by allowing the pipeline to execute against potentially unverified citations, risking the integrity of the entire dataset.

Spec FR-008 requires the system to 'explicitly flag DFT... entries and exclude them'. Task T020 implements a 'dft_filter.py' to 'exclude entries'. The task description lacks the explicit requirement to 'flag' (log/report) these entries before exclusion, which is a mandatory part of the FR-008 deliverable (traceability of excluded data).
